title: 147. Insertion Sort List
question:
Sort a linked list using insertion sort.


A graphical example of insertion sort. The partial sorted list (black) initially contains only the first element in the list.
With each iteration one element (red) is removed from the input data and inserted in-place into the sorted list
 

Algorithm of Insertion Sort:

  1. Insertion sort iterates, consuming one input element each repetition, and growing a sorted output list.
  2. At each iteration, insertion sort removes one element from the input data, finds the location it belongs within the sorted list, and inserts it there.
  3. It repeats until no input elements remain.


Example 1:

Input: 4->2->1->3
Output: 1->2->3->4

Example 2:

Input: -1->5->3->4->0
Output: -1->0->3->4->5
code: # Definition for singly\u002Dlinked list.\u000A# class ListNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.next \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def insertionSortList(self, head):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type head: ListNode\u000A        :rtype: ListNode\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        dummy \u003D ListNode(\u002D1000000)\u000A        dummy.next \u003D head\u000A        boundary_prev, boundary \u003D dummy, head\u000A        while boundary:\u000A            node \u003D boundary\u000A            boundary \u003D boundary.next\u000A            boundary_prev.next \u003D boundary\u000A            prev, cur, node.next \u003D dummy, dummy.next, None\u000A            while cur and cur !\u003D boundary and cur.val \u003C node.val: prev, cur \u003D cur, cur.next\u000A            tmp \u003D prev.next\u000A            prev.next \u003D node\u000A            node.next \u003D tmp\u000A            if boundary_prev.next !\u003D boundary: boundary_prev \u003D boundary_prev.next\u000A        return dummy.next\u000A
